title: achillea millefolium subsp. pallidotegula
intro: Achillea millefolium subsp. pallidotegula, also known as yarrow, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its fern-like leaves and white or yellowish flowers. Yarrow has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and calm digestive issues. The health benefits of yarrow include its ability to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Yarrow can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used as a topical application for skin issues and as a natural insect repellent.
